description.s: Hobson uses JSON-based import/export for data portability, backup, and development workflows. The import system is smart — it handles dependencies, avoids breaking running code, and applies the minimal set of changes.
await api.export(itemId);  // Downloads one item as JSON
await api.export();  // Downloads all items as JSON array
Exported files include all item fields. The format is the same JSON structure used in
When importing items that reference each other (e.g., a view references its type), the import system sorts items in dependency order:
Parse the JSON (single item or array)
Topologically sort — types before instances, parent types before subtypes
Write items in dependency order so validation passes
This means you can export a set of related items and import them into a fresh system without worrying about ordering.
When an imported item has the same ID as an existing item:
The import overwrites the existing item
This is the standard behavior for updating items from file
After import, the system determines the minimal reload needed:
This means importing an updated view doesn't require a full page reload — only the visible instances of that view re-render.
The standard development workflow with Claude Code:
Import updated files into running Hobson
Changes take effect immediately (with appropriate hot-reload)
Export from Hobson to capture any in-system changes
